content_en:
Despite the deep, sad pain which the Prophet (PBUH) suffered over the loss of his uncle Hamzah and the mutilation of his corpse by the polytheists in a manner that drew tears from the Prophet (PBUH) and broke his heart; despite the fact that the field of battle was littered with the corpses of his Companions, all of whom represented the peak of truth, piety and enlightenment; despite all this, he stood at the corpse of his first envoy, bidding him farewell and weeping bitterly. Nay, the Prophet (PBUH) stood at the remains of Mus*ab Ibn ‘Umair saying, while his eyes were flowing with tears, love and loyalty, "<Among the believers are men who have been true to their covenant with Allah >" (33:23).

Then he gave a sad look at the garment in which he was shrouded and said, “I saw you at Makkah, and there was not a more precious jewel, nor more distinguished one than you, and here you are bare-headed in a garment!" Then the Prophet (PBUH) looked at all the martyrs in the battlefield and said, "The Prophet of Allah witnesses that you are martyrs to Allah on the Day of Resurrection." Then he gathered his living Companions around him and said, "O people, visit them, come to them, and salute them. By Allah, no Muslim will salute them but that they will salute him in return."

Peace be on you, O Mus‘ab. Peace be on you, O Martyrs. Peace and blessings of Allah be upon you!

(2) SALMAAN AL-FAARISIY The Seeker after Truth

From Persia comes our hero this time, and from Persia many came to embrace Islam in the long run, and it made some of them extraordinary, unsurpassable in faith and knowledge in religion and worldly affairs.

It is one of the wonders of Islam and its greatness that it never enters a country on Allah's earth but that it exerts invaluable influence on all its potentialities and forces, bringing forth the latent genius of its people and followers. From there came forth Muslim philosophers, physicians, jurists, astronomers, inventors, and mathematicians.

Behold, they reached all heights, broke all frontiers, until the first era of Islam flourished with great geniuses in all fields of intellectual activity such as administration and science. Verily, they came from various nations, but their religion remained one.

The Prophet (PBUH) had prophesied this blessed spread of his religion. Indeed, he had been so promised by his Almighty Lord. He had pointed to the time, place, and day, and he had seen in his mind's eye the banner of Islam fluttering in all corners of the earth and over the palaces of its earthly rulers.

Salmaan Al-Faarisiy (The Persian) bore witness to this and was firmly connected with what happened. That was on the Day of AlKhandaq (The Trench) in the year A.H. 5, when the leaders of the Jews approached Makkah to stir up the polytheists and form an alliance against the Prophet (PBUH) and the Muslims, asking the polytheists to enter upon a treaty for decisive battle to eradicate this new religion.

The ungodly war was planned: the Quraish army and allies would attack Al-Madiinah from outside, while the Bani Quraidhah would attack from within, behind the ranks of the Muslims, who would then fall prey and be crushed. One day the Prophet (PBUH) and the Muslims were taken unaware by a huge well-armed army marching on Al-Madiinah. The Qur'aan depicts the scene thus: <When they came against you from above you and from below you and your eyes turned away and your hearts reached to your throats, and you imagined vain thoughts about GOD; in that place the believers were tried and shaken most severely > (33:10-11).

Twenty-four thousand fighters under the command of Abu Sufyaan and “Uyainah Ibn Hisn were advancing on Al-Madiinah to storm it and to lay siege to it in order to get rid of Muhammad, his religion, and his Companions. This army did not represent the Quraish alone, for they were in alliance with all the tribes, and all had vested interests that were threatened by Islam. It was a last and decisive attempt embarked on by all the enemies of the Prophet (PBUH), based upon individual, collective, and tribal interests.

The Muslims found themselves in a precarious situation. The Prophet (PBUH) assembled his Companions for consultation. Certainly they were gathered to reach a decision on defense and battle, but how could they put up a defense? And then a long - legged man with flowing hair for whom the Prophet (PBUH) bore great love, Salmaan Al-Faarisiy, held up his head and took a look at AlMadiinah, which was surrounded by hills, mountains, and exposed. open country which could be easily broken through by the enemy.

Salmaan had much experience, in warfare and its tactics in his native Persia. So he proposed to the Prophet (PBUH) something which the Arabs had never seen before in warfare. It was the digging of a trench in the exposed places around Al-Madiinah.

And Allah knows what could have been the position of the Muslims in that battle had they not dug the trench, which was no sooner seen by the Quraish than they were stunned by despair. The forces of the enemy still remained in their tents for a month, unable to take Al-Madiinah, until Allah sent them one night a storm which devastated their tents and tore them asunder.

Then Abu Sufyaan announced to his forces that they should return to where they had come from. They were despondent and frustrated.

During the excavation of the trench, Salmaan took his place among the Muslims while they dug and removed the sand. The Prophet (PBUH) was also taking part in digging where Salmaan was working in a group. Their pickaxes could not smash a stubborn rock, in spite of the fact that Salmaan was of strong build and hardworking. A single stroke of his would break a rock to pieces, but he stood in front of this stubborn one. He let all those around him try to break it, but in vain. Salmaan went to the Prophet (PBUH) to ask him to divert the trench around that stubborn and challenging rock.

The Prophet (PBUH) returned with Salmaan to see the rock himself. When he saw it, he called for a pickax and asked the Companions to keep back from the splinters. He said, "In the name of Allah," and then raised his blessed, firm hands gripping the pickax and let it fall.

The rock broke, making a great light. Salmaan said that he himself saw that light shining upon Al-Madiinah. The Prophet (PBUH) raised the pickax and gave a second blow and the rock broke more. At that moment the Prophet (PBUH) said loudly,"Allahu akbar

— Allah is the Greatest — I have been given the keys to Rome; its red palaces have been lit for me and my nation has vanquished it.”

The Prophet (PBUH) struck his third blow. Then the rock shattered and its glittering light was seen! The Prophet (PBUH) told them that he was now looking at the palaces of Syria, San‘aa’ and others like them, and the cities of the world over which the banner of Islam would flutter one day. The Muslims shouted in deep faith, "This is what Allah and His Prophet have promised us!"

Salmaan was the originator of the project to dig the trench, and he was associated with the rock out of which poured some secrets of the unseen and of destiny. When he called the Prophet (PBUH) to break it, he stood by the side of the Prophet (PBUH), saw the light, and heard the glad omen, and he lived to see the prophecy fulfilled and abided in its living reality. He saw the great capitals of Persia and Rome (Byzantium), the palaces of San’aa’, Syria, Egypt, and Iraq. He saw every place trembling with the blessed ecstasy which was issuing forth from the high minarets in all parts of the world, spreading the light of guidance and goodness.

And here he is sitting there in the shade of a tree before his house in Al-Madiinah telling his guests about his great adventures in the quest for truth, explaining to them how he abandoned the religion of his Persian people for Christianity and then for Islam. How he abandoned his father's wealth and estate and threw himself into the arms of the wilderness in the quest for the release of his tension and soul. How he was sold in a slave market on his way to search for truth. How he met with the Prophet (PBUH) and how he came to believe in him. Now let us approach his great court and listen to his grand tale which he is recounting.

I come from Isfahan, from a place called Jai, and I was the most

beloved son of my father, who was a figure of high esteem among his people. We used to worship fire. I devoted myself to fire worship

until I became custodian of the fire which we lit and never allowed to be extinguished.

My father had an estate. One day, he sent me there. I passed by a Christian church and heard them praying. I went in and saw what they were doing. I was impressed by what I saw in their prayers. I said, "This is better than our religion." I did not leave them until sunset, nor did I go to my father's estate, nor did I return to my father until he sent people to search for me.

I asked the Christians about their affair and prayers which impressed me, and about the origin of their religion. They answered, "In Syria." I said to my father when I returned to him, "I passed by people praying in a church of theirs, and I was impressed by their prayer, and I could see that their religion is better than ours." He questioned me and | questioned him, and then he put fetters on my feet and locked me up.

Then I sent to the Christians saying I had entered their religion, and I requested that whenever a caravan came from Syria, they should tell me before its return in order for me to travel with them, and so they did.

I broke loose from the iron fetters and went away. I set out with them for Syria. While I was there, I asked about their learned man, and I was told that he was the bishop, leader of the church. I went to him and told him my story. I lived with him, serving, praying, and learning.

But this bishop was not faithful in his religion, because he used to gather money from the people to distribute it, but he would keep it for himself. Then he died.
content_id:
Meskipun rasa sakit yang dalam dan menyedihkan yang dialami Nabi (SAW) atas wafatnya pamannya Hamzah dan mutilasi jasadnya oleh kaum musyrik dengan cara yang membuat Nabi (SAW) menangis dan menghancurkan hatinya; meskipun medan perang dipenuhi jasad para Sahabatnya, yang semuanya mewakili puncak kebenaran, ketakwaan, dan pencerahan; meskipun semua ini, beliau berdiri di depan jasad utusan pertamanya, mengucapkan selamat tinggal dan menangis dengan getir. Bahkan, Nabi (SAW) berdiri di depan jenazah Mush'ab ibn 'Umair sambil berkata, dengan air mata, cinta, dan kesetiaan mengalir, "<Di antara orang-orang mukmin terdapat orang-orang yang menepati apa yang telah mereka janjikan kepada Allah>" (33:23).

Kemudian beliau menatap dengan sedih kain kafan yang menutupinya dan berkata, "Aku melihatmu di Makkah, dan tidak ada permata yang lebih berharga maupun yang lebih mulia darimu, dan kini engkau berbaring tanpa penutup kepala dalam selembar kain!" Kemudian Nabi (SAW) memandang seluruh para syuhada di medan perang dan berkata, "Nabi Allah bersaksi bahwa kalian adalah para syuhada di sisi Allah pada Hari Kebangkitan." Lalu beliau mengumpulkan para Sahabatnya yang masih hidup di sekelilingnya dan berkata, "Wahai manusia, ziarahilah mereka, datangilah mereka, dan ucapkan salam kepada mereka. Demi Allah, tidaklah seorang Muslim mengucapkan salam kepada mereka melainkan mereka akan membalas salamnya."

Semoga keselamatan tercurah atasmu, wahai Mush'ab. Semoga keselamatan tercurah atas kalian, wahai para Syuhada. Semoga rahmat dan keberkahan Allah tercurah atas kalian!

(2) SALMAAN AL-FAARISIY Sang Pencari Kebenaran

Dari Persia datang pahlawan kita kali ini, dan dari Persia banyak yang akhirnya memeluk Islam, dan hal itu menjadikan sebagian dari mereka luar biasa, tak tertandingi dalam iman dan ilmu agama maupun urusan dunia.

Inilah salah satu keajaiban Islam dan keagungannya bahwa ia tidak pernah memasuki suatu negeri di bumi Allah melainkan memberikan pengaruh yang tak ternilai pada seluruh potensi dan kekuatannya, mengeluarkan kejeniusan tersembunyi dari rakyat dan para pengikutnya. Dari sanalah lahir para filosof, dokter, ahli fiqh, astronom, penemu, dan ahli matematika Muslim.

Lihatlah, mereka meraih segala puncak, menembus segala batas, hingga era pertama Islam berkembang dengan genius-genius besar di semua bidang aktivitas intelektual seperti pemerintahan dan ilmu pengetahuan. Sesungguhnya, mereka datang dari berbagai bangsa, namun agama mereka tetap satu.

Nabi (SAW) telah meramalkan penyebaran agamanya yang penuh berkah ini. Sungguh, hal itu telah dijanjikan oleh Tuhannya Yang Maha Kuasa. Beliau telah menunjukkan waktu, tempat, dan harinya, dan beliau telah melihat dengan mata pikirannya panji Islam berkibar di setiap sudut bumi dan di atas istana para penguasanya.

Salmaan Al-Faarisiy (Orang Persia) menjadi saksi atas hal ini dan terhubung erat dengan apa yang terjadi. Hal itu terjadi pada Hari Al-Khandaq (Parit) pada tahun 5 H, ketika para pemimpin Yahudi mendekati Makkah untuk menghasut kaum musyrik dan membentuk persekutuan melawan Nabi (SAW) dan kaum Muslimin, meminta kaum musyrik untuk mengikat perjanjian demi perang yang menentukan guna memusnahkan agama baru ini.

Perang yang penuh kejahatan itu pun direncanakan: pasukan Quraisy dan sekutunya akan menyerang Al-Madinah dari luar, sementara Bani Quraidhah akan menyerang dari dalam, dari belakang barisan kaum Muslimin, yang kemudian akan menjadi mangsa dan dihancurkan. Suatu hari Nabi (SAW) dan kaum Muslimin tiba-tiba dihadapkan pada pasukan besar yang bersenjata lengkap yang sedang bergerak menuju Al-Madinah. Al-Qur'an menggambarkan suasana itu: <Ketika mereka datang menyerang kalian dari atas dan dari bawah kalian, dan ketika pandangan mata kalian menjadi kabur dan hati kalian menyesak sampai ke kerongkongan, dan kalian menyangka yang bukan-bukan tentang Allah; di situlah orang-orang mukmin diuji dan diguncangkan dengan guncangan yang sangat dahsyat> (33:10-11).

Dua puluh empat ribu pejuang di bawah komando Abu Sufyan dan 'Uyainah ibn Hishn sedang bergerak menuju Al-Madinah untuk menyerbunya dan mengepungnya guna menyingkirkan Muhammad, agamanya, dan para Sahabatnya. Pasukan ini tidak hanya mewakili Quraisy saja, karena mereka bersekutu dengan semua kabilah, dan semua memiliki kepentingan yang terancam oleh Islam. Ini adalah upaya terakhir dan menentukan yang dilakukan oleh semua musuh Nabi (SAW), berdasarkan kepentingan individu, kolektif, dan kesukuan.

Kaum Muslimin mendapati diri mereka dalam situasi yang genting. Nabi (SAW) mengumpulkan para Sahabatnya untuk bermusyawarah. Tentu saja mereka berkumpul untuk mengambil keputusan tentang pertahanan dan peperangan, tetapi bagaimana mereka bisa membangun pertahanan? Lalu seorang pria bertubuh jangkung berambut panjang yang sangat dicintai Nabi (SAW), Salmaan Al-Faarisiy, mengangkat kepalanya dan memandang Al-Madinah, yang dikelilingi oleh bukit-bukit, gunung-gunung, dan tanah terbuka yang mudah ditembus oleh musuh.

Salmaan memiliki banyak pengalaman dalam perang dan taktiknya di tanah airnya Persia. Maka ia mengusulkan kepada Nabi (SAW) sesuatu yang belum pernah dilihat oleh orang Arab sebelumnya dalam peperangan. Yaitu penggalian parit di tempat-tempat terbuka di sekitar Al-Madinah.

Dan Allah mengetahui bagaimana keadaan kaum Muslimin dalam pertempuran itu seandainya mereka tidak menggali parit tersebut, yang begitu dilihat oleh Quraisy langsung membuat mereka terpukul oleh keputusasaan. Pasukan musuh masih bertahan di tenda-tenda mereka selama sebulan, tidak mampu memasuki Al-Madinah, hingga Allah mengirimkan kepada mereka suatu malam badai yang menghancurkan dan merobek tenda-tenda mereka.

Kemudian Abu Sufyan mengumumkan kepada pasukannya agar mereka kembali ke tempat asal mereka. Mereka pergi dalam keadaan putus asa dan frustrasi.

Selama penggalian parit, Salmaan mengambil tempatnya di antara kaum Muslimin yang menggali dan memindahkan pasir. Nabi (SAW) pun turut serta dalam penggalian di tempat Salmaan bekerja dalam satu kelompok. Kapak-kapak mereka tidak mampu menghancurkan sebuah batu yang keras, meskipun Salmaan bertubuh kuat dan pekerja keras. Satu ayunan tangannya biasanya mampu membelah batu menjadi kepingan-kepingan, namun ia berdiri di hadapan batu yang keras ini. Ia mempersilakan semua orang di sekitarnya untuk mencoba menghancurkannya, namun sia-sia. Salmaan pergi menemui Nabi (SAW) untuk meminta beliau mengalihkan parit melingkari batu yang keras dan menantang itu.

Nabi (SAW) kembali bersama Salmaan untuk melihat batu itu sendiri. Ketika beliau melihatnya, beliau meminta kapak dan meminta para Sahabat untuk menjauh dari serpihan. Beliau berkata, "Bismillah," lalu mengangkat tangannya yang kuat dan penuh berkah yang menggenggam kapak dan membiarkannya jatuh.

Batu itu terbelah, memancarkan cahaya yang sangat terang. Salmaan berkata bahwa ia sendiri melihat cahaya itu bersinar menerangi Al-Madinah. Nabi (SAW) mengangkat kapak dan memberikan pukulan kedua dan batu itu semakin terbelah. Pada saat itu Nabi (SAW) bersabda dengan keras, "Allahu Akbar

— Allah Maha Besar — Aku telah diberikan kunci-kunci Roma; istana-istana merahnya telah diterangi untukku dan umatku telah mengalahkannya."

Nabi (SAW) memberikan pukulan ketiga. Lalu batu itu hancur dan cahaya gemerlapnya pun terlihat! Nabi (SAW) memberitahu mereka bahwa beliau kini melihat istana-istana Syam, San'a' dan kota-kota lainnya yang serupa, serta kota-kota di seluruh dunia yang suatu hari akan berkibar panji Islam di atasnya. Kaum Muslimin berseru dengan iman yang teguh, "Inilah yang telah dijanjikan Allah dan Rasul-Nya kepada kami!"

Salmaan adalah penggagas proyek penggalian parit, dan ia terhubung dengan batu yang darinya terpancar beberapa rahasia gaib dan takdir. Ketika ia memanggil Nabi (SAW) untuk menghancurkannya, ia berdiri di sisi Nabi (SAW), melihat cahaya, dan mendengar kabar gembira itu, dan ia hidup untuk menyaksikan nubuat itu terwujud dan menetap dalam kenyataan hidupnya. Ia menyaksikan ibu kota-ibu kota besar Persia dan Roma (Byzantium), istana-istana San'a', Syam, Mesir, dan Irak. Ia menyaksikan setiap tempat bergetar dengan ekstasi penuh berkah yang mengalir dari menara-menara tinggi di seluruh penjuru dunia, menyebarkan cahaya petunjuk dan kebaikan.

Dan kini ia duduk di bawah naungan sebuah pohon di depan rumahnya di Al-Madinah menceritakan kepada para tamunya tentang petualangan besarnya dalam pencarian kebenaran, menjelaskan kepada mereka bagaimana ia meninggalkan agama orang-orang Persia untuk memeluk Kristen kemudian Islam. Bagaimana ia meninggalkan harta dan tanah milik ayahnya dan melemparkan dirinya ke pelukan belantara dalam pencarian kebebasan bagi jiwa dan batinnya. Bagaimana ia dijual di pasar budak dalam perjalanannya mencari kebenaran. Bagaimana ia bertemu dengan Nabi (SAW) dan bagaimana ia beriman kepadanya. Kini marilah kita mendekati majlisnya yang agung dan mendengarkan kisah besarnya yang sedang ia ceritakan.

Aku berasal dari Isfahan, dari sebuah tempat bernama Jai, dan aku adalah anak yang paling

dicintai ayahku, yang merupakan sosok terhormat di kalangan kaumnya. Kami biasa menyembah api. Aku mencurahkan diriku dalam ibadah api

hingga aku menjadi penjaga api yang kami nyalakan dan tidak pernah kami biarkan padam.

Ayahku memiliki sebidang tanah. Suatu hari, ia mengutusku ke sana. Dalam perjalanan, aku melewati sebuah gereja Kristen dan mendengar mereka berdoa. Aku masuk dan menyaksikan apa yang mereka lakukan. Aku terkesan dengan apa yang kulihat dalam doa-doa mereka. Aku berkata, "Ini lebih baik dari agama kita." Aku tidak meninggalkan mereka hingga matahari terbenam, tidak pula pergi ke tanah milik ayahku, dan tidak pula kembali kepada ayahku hingga ia mengutus orang-orang untuk mencari aku.

Aku bertanya kepada orang-orang Kristen tentang urusan mereka dan doa-doa yang mengesankanku, serta tentang asal-usul agama mereka. Mereka menjawab, "Di Syam." Aku berkata kepada ayahku ketika aku kembali kepadanya, "Aku melewati orang-orang yang sedang berdoa di sebuah gereja milik mereka, dan aku terkesan dengan doa mereka, dan aku dapat melihat bahwa agama mereka lebih baik dari agama kita." Ia menanyaiku dan aku pun menanyainya, kemudian ia memasang belenggu di kakiku dan mengurungku.

Lalu aku mengirim pesan kepada orang-orang Kristen bahwa aku telah memeluk agama mereka, dan aku meminta agar setiap kali ada kafilah yang datang dari Syam, mereka memberitahuku sebelum kafilah itu kembali agar aku bisa ikut bersama mereka, dan mereka pun melakukannya.

Aku melepaskan diri dari belenggu besi itu dan pergi. Aku berangkat bersama mereka menuju Syam. Sesampainya di sana, aku bertanya tentang orang alim mereka, dan aku diberitahu bahwa dia adalah uskup, pemimpin gereja. Aku mendatanginya dan menceritakan kisahku. Aku tinggal bersamanya, melayani, berdoa, dan belajar.

Namun uskup ini tidak jujur dalam agamanya, karena ia biasa mengumpulkan harta dari masyarakat untuk dibagikan, tetapi ia menyimpannya untuk dirinya sendiri. Kemudian ia meninggal dunia.
